title: Causal Observability, Freshness SLOs, Capacity, and Cost · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Capacity Model
summary: Logs contain feed bodies and tokens, traces lose attempt identity, averages hide stale publishers, metrics explode by URL, and load tests ignore PostgreSQL and origin budgets. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go capacity model increment in privacy-minimized feed-service operations report. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against CPU, memory, connections, locks, and budgets.
Bound metric labels, trace sampling, event retention, tenant exposure, and telemetry cost while retaining incident usefulness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Capacity Model · Theory Feed Go Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9599 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove privacy-minimized causal telemetry, freshness SLOs, bounded cardinality, and representative capacity; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against CPU, memory, connections, locks, and budgets.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Feed Go Capacity Model contract for the supplied data.
